instructions.content: Ask exactly ONE question per turn — never bundle multiple questions into a single call, even if the question tool nominally supports several at once.
Every question must offer 3-4 concrete, mutually exclusive options, each with a short description of its implication or tradeoff, plus an always-open free-text path.
Use the previous answer to decide the next question — skip questions the answer made moot, add questions a new branch opened. Do not work through a fixed list regardless of answers.
Recap every answer in a short bulleted summary and get explicit confirmation before producing any final output.
NEVER offer fewer than 3 or more than 4 options on a question that has a real decision space — split an overloaded question into two instead.
NEVER produce a written file the user did not ask for and that isn't clearly needed by downstream work — default to a chat answer.
Skip this skill entirely for purely factual questions with one right answer or requests that are already unambiguous.
